exclusions.reason: Saving state implies edit just confirmed; trigger is hidden during async save
Edit trigger is suppressed during saving; spinner replaces it
Disabled blocks all interaction; edit mode is unreachable from disabled state
Error only manifests in edit mode with inline validation; view state shows last valid value
Blur-to-save with required conflicts: tabbing away from empty field would silently fail or block navigation

_decisions.what: State as variant with 5 values: view, edit, saving, error, disabled
_decisions.why: Each state changes the component structure substantially: view shows readView, edit shows input+buttons, saving shows spinner, error shows validation message, disabled shows locked readView — all ≥2 visual property changes → variant required

_decisions.what: Disabled is a boolean AND a State value
_decisions.why: Disabled changes structural layout (locked indicator replaces edit trigger, removes interaction affordances) — ≥2 visual properties change → variant per Rule 27. Boolean is also retained for code interface alignment (isDisabled prop)

_decisions.what: TriggerVariant kept as variant dimension (not boolean)
_decisions.why: hover-reveal vs. always-visible changes the layout of the readView slot (pencil icon always present vs. hidden until hover) — structural difference visible in Figma frames

_decisions.what: ConfirmModel (explicit / blur-to-save) removed from variant matrix
_decisions.why: ConfirmModel is a behavioral/config prop, not a visual structural difference; both models show the same confirm/cancel buttons in edit state — managed as SubmitOnBlur boolean
Validation message slot needs both a visibility toggle (Show Validation bool) and editable text content — boolText is the correct figmaBinding for paired bool+text
editInput is a composed text-input sub-component (not plain text); it's an instance swap region that accepts a TextField instance, not a raw text layer

_decisions.what: confirmButton and cancelButton use bool figmaBinding
_decisions.why: These are simple icon-action buttons that appear/disappear based on State; their visibility is toggled as boolean layers
5 states × 3 sizes = 15 gross; minus 3 invalid state×size combos = 12 net; InlineEdit is a simple single-slot form control despite its state machine complexity
InlineEdit is a form-control archetype; input-like components use rectangular radius (4px) not pill shape

_decisions.what: SubmitOnBlur defaults to false per a11y consensus
_decisions.why: Blur-to-save is an accessibility risk for keyboard and switch users; Atlassian and Polaris both recommend explicit confirmation as default
